title: ActiveMQ Transport Connector Accept Failure
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder file monitor + `props.conf` sourcetype override; JMS Messaging Modular Input (Splunkbase 1317) for client-plane troubleshooting context
spl:
index=messaging sourcetype="activemq:log"
| search ("TransportConnector" OR "Connector") (ERROR OR WARN OR FATAL)
| search "failed" OR "stopped" OR "stopped accepting" OR "IOException"
| stats latest(_raw) as sample by host
| eval connector_down=1
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable: this use case is not built on a CIM data model. Rely on the search in Step 2 against your raw logs or metrics.
description: Connector-level WARN/ERROR lines (bind failures, accept loop stopped, I/O exceptions) mean clients cannot open new sessions on that transport even if the JVM is still running.
value: Surfaces partial outages—one OpenWire/AMQP/STOMP listener down—that JVM uptime checks miss.
grandmaExplanation: We use this to surfaces partial outages — one OpenWire/AMQP/STOMP listener down — that JVM uptime checks miss.
implementation: Forward `activemq.log` from each broker. Normalize multi-line stack traces. Maintain a lookup of known maintenance hosts. Correlate with OS firewall and TLS keystore rotations.
visualization: Timeline (connector errors), Table (host, sample message), Single value (affected brokers).
knownFalsePositives: Queues and broker metrics swing during rebalancing, replay, or maintenance. We align with change windows.
